This evolution, together with the collaborative nature of robotics projects, has made the development, integration, deployment, and long-term operation of robotic systems significantly more complex. In practice, multi-user robotics software teams face persistent challenges related to dependency isolation, compatibility, reproducibility, efficient sharing of specialized hardware, and deployment across heterogeneous environments. In this paper, we present CSAR (Containerized System Architecture for Robotics), a container-centric architectural framework designed specifically for robotics teams and the edge-cloud continuum. CSAR combines LXC/LXD-based system containerization, ROS 2/DDS-based communication, and a three-layer edge infrastructure to organize computation into hardware-affine, persistent execution environments that remain decoupled from the volatility of experimental workloads. Through its Infrastructure Core, Platform and Multi-User Orchestration, and Compute and Acceleration layers, CSAR provides strong isolation, controlled resource sharing, and topology-aware networking for distributed robotic applications. To demonstrate its validity, we describe a real deployment of CSAR in an academic robotics laboratory and evaluate it through representative use cases involving edge-offloaded 3D SLAM and GPU-accelerated semantic mapping. The results indicate that CSAR simplifies software integration, improves the utilization of shared computational resources, and facilitates safe prototyping, as well as reproducible and collaborative experimentation in robotics teams. The implementation described in this paper, including deployment templates, configuration files, and documentation, is available at https://github.com/goyoambrosio/CSAR.

</details>


### [478] [Chronos: A Physics-Informed Full-History Framework for Non-Markovian Long-Horizon Manipulation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30318)
*Yulin Zhou,Yimeng Wang,Nengyu Wang,Shaojia Xing,Shiyun Tu,Xiang Li,Jingkai Zhang,Ningbo Jiang,Yuankai Lin,Hua Yang,Xiangrui Zeng,Zhouping Yin*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: Chronos提出了一个全历史框架，将观测历史作为策略的潜状态，通过选择性状态空间模型和二阶薛定谔桥，在非马尔可夫长时程操作任务上大幅超越现有方法（如pi0.5提升6.6倍），且参数量减少10-30倍。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 当前通用机器人策略（如VLA和生成式模仿策略）大多基于当前观测或短时间窗口建模，这种马尔可夫捷径在需要记忆的操作任务中失败——相同观测在不同历史后需要不同动作。因此需要一种非马尔可夫的全历史框架。

Method: 将观测历史提升为策略动力学的潜状态：1）每个控制步将观测和本体感觉融合成一个状态表征token，与物理时间一一对齐；2）选择性状态空间模型传播因果历史状态；3）通过隐式最大似然估计（IMLE）条件化多模态粗动作先验；4）二阶薛定谔启发桥（Schrodinger bridge）预测加速度场，精细化动作，产生更平滑、物理合理的运动。

Result: 在16个模拟任务和4个真实世界实验中评估：RMBench上达到73.6%平均成功率，超越马尔可夫基线pi0.5（+62.4个百分点，6.6倍提升），参数量少10倍；超越记忆VLA Mem-0 22.8个百分点，参数量少30倍。真实双臂实验中，Chronos平均成功率78%（记忆依赖任务72%），pi0.5仅7%（记忆依赖任务0%）。

Conclusion: 历史不应被视为辅助上下文，而应作为操作策略的潜状态。Chronos通过物理启发的全历史建模，在记忆依赖的长时程操作任务上实现了显著的性能提升和参数效率。

Abstract: General-purpose robot policies should be modeled as dynamical systems, yet many VLA and generative imitation policies still rely on present observations or short windows. This Markovian shortcut fails in memory-dependent manipulation: identical observations can demand different actions after different histories. We present Chronos, a physics-informed full-history framework for non-Markovian long-horizon manipulation. The key idea is to elevate observation history from auxiliary context to the latent state of the policy dynamics. At each physical control step, Chronos forms one state-representative token by fusing observation and proprioception, so the token sequence is aligned one-to-one with physical time. A selective state space model propagates this causal historical state, which conditions a multimodal coarse action prior through implicit maximum likelihood estimation (IMLE). This prior is then refined by a second-order Schrodinger-inspired bridge that predicts acceleration fields, yielding smoother and more physically grounded robot motion. Across 16 simulated tasks and 4 real-world experiments, Chronos is evaluated on precision insertion, general manipulation, and memory-dependent long-horizon control. On RMBench, where success requires remembering task phase, Chronos achieves 73.6% average success, outperforming Markovian VLA baseline pi0.5 by +62.4 percentage points, a 6.6x relative gain, while using 10x fewer parameters. It also surpasses the memory VLA Mem-0 by 22.8 points while using over 30x fewer parameters. In real-world dual-arm experiments using a single RGB camera, Chronos achieves 78% average success over four tasks, including 72% on the three memory-dependent tasks, whereas pi0.5 achieves 7% overall and 0% on the memory-dependent subset. These results suggest that history should not be treated as auxiliary context, but as the latent state of the manipulation policy.

</details>


### [479] [ReactiveBFM: Reactive Closed-Loop Motion Planning Towards Universal Humanoid Whole-Body Control](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30362)
*Xiao Chen,Weishuai Zeng,Xiaojie Niu,Zirui Wang,Jianan Li,Huayi Wang,Furui Xu,Jiahe Chen,Weixiang Zhong,Lihe Ding,Kailin Li,Jiangmiao Pang,Tai Wang,Tianfan Xue,Jingbo Wang*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 提出ReactiveBFM，一种实时闭环规划-控制框架，通过调度前缀采样课程缓解曝光偏差，并利用异步重规划机制解决延迟不匹配问题，在Unitree G1人形机器人上实现了零样本运动目标追踪和93.1%的成功率。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有行为基础模型(BFM)仅能执行预定义参考运动，对环境变化脆弱且无法实现反应性全身协调；直接级联生成式运动规划器会因跟踪偏差导致致命的累积曝光偏差。

Method: 提出调度前缀采样课程(scheduled prefix sampling curriculum)让生成式规划器主动从非完美物理状态中学习纠错行为；引入异步重规划机制(asynchronous replanning)配合轨迹分块(trajectory chunking)解决自回归规划与高频跟踪之间的延迟不匹配。

Result: 在Unitree G1人形机器人上实现零样本移动目标追踪和即时重规划；在严重扰动下的sim-to-sim基准测试中达到93.1%成功率，比级联开环基线高出28.6%。

Conclusion: ReactiveBFM通过闭环规划-控制框架有效弥合了生成式运动规划与物理跟踪之间的鸿沟，实现了前所未有的物理敏捷性和反应性全身协调能力。

Abstract: While current Behavior Foundation Models (BFMs) provide robust control priors for humanoids, they only execute pre-defined reference motions. As a result, they are vulnerable to environmental shifts and incapable of reactive whole-body coordination. Naively cascading them with generative motion planners fails to achieve true reactivity, as inevitable tracking discrepancies induce fatal cumulative exposure bias. To bridge this gap, we propose ReactiveBFM, a real-time closed-loop planning-control framework. At its core, we effectively mitigate exposure bias via a scheduled prefix sampling curriculum, forcing the generative planner to actively learn error-recovery behaviors from imperfect physical states rather than ground-truth trajectories. Systematically, to reconcile the severe latency mismatch between auto-regressive planning and high-frequency tracking, we introduce an asynchronous replanning mechanism. Combined with trajectory chunking to temporally ensemble spatial references, our system guarantees spatio-temporally fluid execution without physical jitter. Deployed on the Unitree G1 humanoid, ReactiveBFM demonstrates unprecedented physical agility across a vast repertoire of text-conditioned closed-loop motions. Notably, ReactiveBFM achieves zero-shot moving target reaching, showcasing intricate whole-body coordination and on-the-fly replanning. In sim-to-sim benchmarking under severe perturbations, ReactiveBFM achieves a 93.1% success rate, significantly outperforming cascaded open-loop baselines by 28.6%.

</details>


### [480] [FutureNav: Unified World-Action Modeling for Vision-and-Language Navigation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30367)
*Lingfeng Zhang,Zeying Gong,Xiaoshuai Hao,Haoxiang Fu,Qiang Zhang,Mingliang Zhou,Hangjun Ye,Xiaojun Liang,Junwei Liang,Wenbo Ding*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: FutureNav提出了一种统一的世界-动作建模框架，通过联合优化四个目标（动作策略、逆动力学、前向动力学和未来状态生成），在连续环境视觉语言导航中以4B规模模型实现了SOTA性能。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有导航基础模型虽然通过扩展视觉-语言模型取得了进展，但通常将导航学习视为直接的行动生成，没有显式建模世界状态或预测其未来演变，导致缺乏对环境的深层理解。

Method: FutureNav构建了统一的VLM框架，联合编码文本、视觉和空间特征输入LLM，同时优化四个目标：动作策略目标（导航动作预测）、逆动力学目标（从当前状态和动作推断前状态）、前向动力学目标（从当前状态和动作预测下一状态）、未来生成目标（预测未来空间状态）。

Result: 在多个VLN基准上，仅使用4B规模参数即达到了最先进的性能，显著优于先前的VLN方法，且推理速度未受影响。

Conclusion: 统一的世界-动作建模能够强化动作预测的同时显式建模世界状态，为未来VLN的世界-动作模型研究铺平了道路。代码和模型将开源。

Abstract: Vision-and-language navigation (VLN) in continuous environments requires an agent to ground instructions in egocentric observations while maintaining spatial understanding across long action sequences. Recent navigation foundation models have shown strong progress by scaling vision-language models, but they often learn navigation primarily as direct action generation, without explicitly modeling world states or predicting their future evolution. We introduce FutureNav, a VLM-based unified world-action modeling framework for vision-and-language navigation. Specifically, FutureNav jointly encodes text, visual, and spatial features and feeds them into the LLM, and optimizes four objectives for simultaneous world and action modeling: an action policy objective for navigation action prediction, inverse and forward dynamics objectives for modeling state transitions, and a future generation objective for predicting future spatial states. This unified architecture strengthens action prediction while explicitly modeling the world, without sacrificing inference speed. Extensive experiments show that, with only a 4B-scale backbone, FutureNav achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple VLN benchmarks and substantially outperforms prior VLN methods, paving the way toward future world-action models for VLN. We will release the code and models to support future research.

</details>


### [481] [HUMEMBR: Learning Human Routines for Predictive Embodied Navigation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30404)
*Samira Huber,Klaas Pelzer,Duc M. Nguyen,Xuesu Xiao,Sören Pirk*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本文提出HUMEMBR系统，通过持续记忆构建与并行检索机制，使机器人能够基于长期人类行为模式进行推理和导航，在减少token使用量的同时提升了长时程行为推理能力，并在实体机器人上验证了有效性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 机器人在真实环境中需要理解人类行为模式、回答关于特定个体位置和行为规律的问题，但现有方法难以在长时间跨度上高效推理人类行为。

Method: 提出HUMEMBR系统，包含连续记忆构建过程和并行检索与查询机制，能积累人类行为的结构化表征，并支持交互式用户查询。

Result: 相比全上下文LLM基线，HUMEMBR在长时程人类行为推理上表现更好，且token消耗显著减少；在两种真实环境的实体机器人上成功处理了多样查询和导航任务。

Conclusion: HUMEMBR通过结构化记忆机制有效提升了机器人对人类行为的长期理解与推理能力，为现实世界中的具身问答和常规条件导航提供了可行方案。

Abstract: Understanding and navigating human-centered environments over extended periods of time while considering human behavior and routines remains a fundamental challenge in robotics. In real-world settings, robots may be asked to locate a specific individual, predict where that person is likely to be, or estimate when they typically leave a building. Addressing such queries requires reasoning over extensive histories of observations and capturing long-term behavioral patterns. To this end, we introduce Human-Centered Memory for Embodied Robots (HUMEMBR), a system designed for embodied question answering and routine-conditioned navigation. HUMEMBR integrates a continuous memory construction process with a parallel retrieval and querying mechanism, enabling the system to accumulate structured representations of human routines while supporting interactive, user-driven queries. Our experimental results indicate that HUMEMBR improves long-horizon reasoning about human behavior relative to full-context LLM baselines, while using substantially fewer tokens. Furthermore, we deploy HUMEMBR on a physical robot in two distinct environments, showing its ability to handle diverse queries and navigation tasks under real-world conditions.

</details>


### [482] [Vision-Language-Action Models: Experimental Insights from a Real-World UR5 Platform](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30456)
*Mathilde Hochedel,Marc Lalonde*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本项目系统研究了VLA模型从仿真环境迁移到真实UR5e机械臂平台的可行性，构建了完整的数据采集、处理、微调与部署流程，并通过实验揭示了离线性能与真实闭环控制之间存在系统性差距，指出问题根源在于整个数据-模型-控制管线的系统级整合而非模型本身。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有VLA模型主要在受控基准上验证，缺乏从仿真到真实机器人的可复现、可操作的迁移研究，作者旨在填补这一空白，建立评估学习型操作系统的可复现框架。

Method: 集成真实机器人数据采集、RLDS格式数据集工程、OpenVLA与OpenVLA-OFT模型的微调与部署，系统验证动作表示与控制接口，构建完整的数据-模型-控制管线。

Result: 开发了四项基础资产：真实机器人数据采集管线、RLDS标准数据集转换流程、VLA模型微调与推理基础设施、基于真实机器人实验的结构化观察集。实验揭示了离线指标与真实闭环行为之间的持续差距。

Conclusion: VLA系统在真实场景的成功部署更多依赖于对整个数据-模型-控制管线的精确控制，而非模型能力的增量提升；应将问题从模型中心视角重新定义为系统级问题。

Abstract: This project investigates whether recent Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models can be transferred from controlled research benchmarks to a real-world robotic platform, specifically a UR5e manipulator, in a reproducible and operationally meaningful manner. The work integrates real-robot data acquisition, dataset engineering (compatible with the RLDS format), and the fine-tuning and deployment of OpenVLA and OpenVLA-OFT models, with systematic validation of action representations and control interfaces. The project resulted in several foundational assets: (i) a complete real-robot data acquisition pipeline, (ii) a dataset conversion workflow aligned with RLDS standards, (iii) an initial fine-tuning and inference infrastructure for VLA models, and (iv) a structured set of experimental observations grounded in real-robot trials. These elements collectively establish a reproducible framework for evaluating learning-based manipulation systems beyond simulation. Empirically, the experiments reveal a consistent gap between promising offline indicators and unstable closed-loop behavior on the physical system: this gap cannot be attributed solely to model limitations, it is strongly influenced by action semantics, coordinate frame conventions, temporal alignment between modalities, image preprocessing consistency, and dataset coverage and quality. These observations lead to a key interpretation: the successful deployment of VLA systems in real-world settings depends less on incremental improvements in model capacity and more on precise control of the entire data-model-control pipeline. The project reframes VLA-based robotics from a primarily model-centric challenge to a system-level problem; it highlights the difficulty of running robust task execution on the real robot and provides a clear, experimentally grounded understanding of the conditions required for reliable deployment.

</details>


### [483] [Behavior Prompting Policy: Demonstrations as Prompts for Manipulation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30457)
*Austin Patel,Ben Pekarek,Joel Enrique Castro Hernandez,Shuran Song*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本文提出行为提示（Behavior Prompting）范式，通过单个人类演示（行为提示）让机器人即时执行新任务，无需微调。贡献包括：BPP架构、iPhUMI数据采集接口、DrawAnything和LIBERO-Gen评估基准。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有机器人技能学习方法通常需要大量数据微调或预定义任务集，缺乏灵活应对新任务的能力。作者希望让机器人像大语言模型一样，通过上下文示例（上下文学习）即时适应新任务，降低机器人技能获取的成本和门槛。

Method: 提出行为提示策略（BPP），一种上下文视觉运动架构，将行为提示（单个人类演示）和当前观测映射为机器人动作。数据方面，强调任务多样性是关键，并开发iPhUMI手持式操作接口收集多样训练数据。评估方面，引入DrawAnything（绘图）和LIBERO-Gen（桌面操作）基准。

Result: BPP能够在新任务上通过单个演示实现测试时适应，iPhUMI接口可以高效指定行为提示，使人类通过单次演示即可让机器人完成已知或全新任务。无需昂贵的微调过程。

Conclusion: 行为提示提供了一种灵活可扩展的机器人技能教学范式，通过上下文学习降低了对大量数据和计算资源的需求，为机器人快速适应新任务提供了可行路径。

Abstract: We study behavior prompting, a paradigm that enables robots to perform new tasks at inference time given a single human demonstration, which we call a behavior prompt. To enable this capability, we present contributions in algorithm, data, and evaluation. For algorithm, we introduce Behavior Prompting Policy (BPP), an in-context visuomotor architecture that translates the behavior prompt and the current observation into robot actions. For data, we identify that task diversity is the primary driver of the prompting capability and introduce iPhUMI, a handheld manipulation interface for collecting diverse training data. For evaluation, we introduce DrawAnything and LIBERO-Gen to evaluate test-time adaptation to unseen drawing and tabletop manipulation tasks. We also demonstrate that iPhUMI serves as a practical interface for specifying behavior prompts at test time, enabling a human to command a robot via a single demonstration to complete known tasks or to define new robot capabilities. Altogether, behavior prompting provides a flexible and scalable way to teach robots new skills without the need for expensive fine-tuning. Our project website is located at https://behavior-prompting.github.io/ .

</details>


### [484] [Grasp-Oriented Non-Prehensile Manipulation via Learning a Graspability Field](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30474)
*Licheng Zhong,Gim Hee Lee*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本文提出了一种基于可抓取性目标优化的非prehensile操作框架，无需预定义目标姿态，通过强化学习实现从操作到抓取的闭环策略。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有非prehensile操作方法通常需要预设目标物体姿态，但实际中物体存在多种可抓取构型，理想姿态事先未知。

Method: 构建可抓取集合并定义抓取性场，衡量物体构型对成功抓取的适宜程度，利用标量值作为稠密学习信号进行强化学习，同时决定何时终止操作。

Result: 仿真与真实机器人实验表明，策略能可靠地将物体重配置到可抓取状态并过渡到抓取，无需外部规划器或手动停止条件。预测的抓取性距离与真实抓取成功相关性良好。

Conclusion: 该工作验证了通过学习物体构型的抓取可行性表征，可实现无预设目标的闭环操作-抓取一体化策略。

Abstract: Non-prehensile manipulation is often used as a preparatory step for robotic grasping, yet existing approaches typically require a predefined target object pose. In practice, however, objects admit multiple graspable configurations and the desired pose is not known in advance. We reformulate non-prehensile manipulation for grasping as optimizing an object centric graspability objective rather than reaching a specific pose. We construct a graspable set from synthesized grasps and define a graspability field that measures how suitable an object configuration is for successful grasp execution. The scalar measure provides a dense learning signal for reinforcement learning and determines when to terminate manipulation. This yields a closed-loop manipulation-to-grasp pipeline driven by a single policy. Experiments in simulation and on a real robot show that the policy reliably reconfigures objects into graspable states and transitions to grasping without external planners or manually specified stopping conditions. The predicted graspability distance correlates with real world grasp success, which indicates that the learned representation captures grasp feasibility of object configurations.

</details>


### [485] [Learning from Mistakes: Rollout-Retrieval Lifelong Policy Learning for Autonomous Driving](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30537)
*Cheng Gong,Haoyang Wang,Chao Lu,Zirui Li,Jianwei Gong*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本文提出R²LPL框架，通过从策略自身可恢复的错误中提取纠正性知识，并在终身学习框架中持续改进自动驾驶策略，仅需少量滚动-检索循环即可将中等性能的规划器提升至SOTA水平。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有自动驾驶策略大多依赖专家演示训练后靠泛化处理闭环场景，缺乏明确的错误纠正与知识保留机制，无法持续从部署中遇到的多样化长尾场景中改进。

Method: 提出R²LPL（Rollout-Retrieval Lifelong Policy Learning），通过过滤可恢复的错误状态并检索可行的纠正目标，将稀疏失败证据转化为紧凑的监督知识，用于终身策略学习。

Result: 在nuPlan基准上，经过少量滚动-终身学习循环，R²LPL将中等初始性能的学习型规划器提升至SOTA水平，尤其在挑战性的Test14-hard长尾场景上表现突出。

Conclusion: R²LPL有效实现了将可恢复的闭环错误转化为纠正性知识，从而支持自动驾驶策略的持续改进。

Abstract: Autonomous driving policies should be able to improve continually as deployment exposes them to increasingly diverse and long-tail traffic situations. However, most learning-based policies are trained or fine-tuned on expert demonstrations and then rely largely on generalization to handle challenging closed-loop scenarios, lacking an explicit mechanism to correct and retain the mistakes exposed in these scenarios. This paper studies autonomous driving policy improvement from a lifelong learning perspective: Can a pretrained policy improve continually by accumulating corrective knowledge derived from its own mistakes, while retaining previously acquired driving competence? To answer this question, we propose Rollout-Retrieval Lifelong Policy Learning (R$^2$LPL), a policy learning framework that retrieves corrective targets from recoverable policy-induced mistakes and retains the resulting knowledge through lifelong policy learning. R^2LPL addresses a key bottleneck in continual policy improvement: closed-loop mistakes reveal where the policy is weak, but do not directly specify what the policy should learn. By filtering recoverable mistake-related states and retrieving feasible corrective targets, R$^2$LPL turns sparse failure evidence into compact supervised knowledge for stable and sample-efficient policy improvement. We evaluate R$^2$LPL on large-scale closed-loop nuPlan benchmarks. With only a few rollout and continual-learning cycles, R$^2$LPL elevates a learning-based planner with moderate initial performance to state-of-the-art performance across the evaluated benchmarks, especially on the challenging and long-tail Test14-hard split. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of R$^2$LPL in converting recoverable closed-loop mistakes into corrective knowledge for sustained policy improvement.

</details>


### [486] [Training Vision-Language-Action Models with Dense Embodied Chain-of-Thought Supervision](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30552)
*Haoyang Li,Guanlin Li,Youhe Feng,Chen Zhao,Zhuoran Wang,Yang Li,Qizhe Wei,Shifeng Bao,Haitao Shen,Yihan Zhao,Tong Yang,Jing Zhang*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: ZR-0是一个26亿参数的跨体现迁移VLA模型，通过密集体现思维链（ECoT）监督和对偶流架构（VLM系统2 + DiT动作专家系统1），在单臂、双臂、人形机器人等跨体现场景中实现强性能，无需推理时执行ECoT。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 跨体现迁移中，低级状态和动作空间在不同机器人平台上存在根本差异，而高层认知过程（场景感知、物体识别、任务规划等）是共享的，因此需要一种能对齐跨体现表征的VLA模型。

Method: 提出ZR-0模型：1）对偶流架构，预训练VLM（系统2）生成结构化ECoT推理，DiT动作专家（系统1）通过流匹配生成连续动作块；2）通过交叉注意力耦合，注意力掩码限制动作专家仅关注输入提示特征，推理时可完全跳过ECoT；3）在ProcCorpus-60M（60M帧，400K+轨迹）上预训练，96.8%帧具有密集ECoT标注。

Result: 在LIBERO（单臂）、RoboTwin 2.0（双臂）、RoboCasa GR-1 Tabletop（人形）三个模拟基准以及xArm平台真实实验中均表现优异。

Conclusion: ZR-0通过密集ECoT监督实现了有效的跨体现迁移，且推理时无需执行ECoT，兼顾了性能与效率。

Abstract: Cross-embodiment transfer in vision-language-action (VLA) models remains challenging because low-level state and action spaces differ fundamentally across robot platforms. We observe that the high-level cognitive process underlying manipulation, including scene perception, object identification, task planning, and sub-task decomposition, is largely shared across embodiments. Based on this observation, we present ZR-0, a 2.6 billion parameter end-to-end VLA model that uses dense Embodied Chain-of-Thought (ECoT) supervision to align cross-embodiment representations within the vision-language model (VLM). ZR-0 adopts a dual-stream architecture: a pre-trained VLM (System 2) generates structured ECoT reasoning during training, while a Diffusion Transformer-based action expert (System 1) produces continuous action chunks via flow matching. The two components are coupled through cross-attention, with an attention mask that restricts the action expert to input prompt features only, enabling ECoT generation to be entirely skipped at inference without any performance loss. ZR-0 is pre-trained on ProcCorpus-60M, a large-scale dataset comprising approximately 60 million frames (approximately 1,000 hours) from over 400K trajectories, with dense ECoT annotations covering 96.8% of all frames. We evaluate ZR-0 on three simulation benchmarks spanning single-arm (LIBERO), bimanual (RoboTwin 2.0), and humanoid (RoboCasa GR-1 Tabletop) embodiments, as well as real-world experiments on the xArm platform, demonstrating strong performance across all settings. Code and model checkpoints are available at https://github.com/RUCKBReasoning/ZR-0.

</details>


### [487] [MOAR Planner: Multi-Objective and Adaptive Risk-Aware Path Planning for Infrastructure Inspection with a UAV](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30575)
*Louis Petit,Alexis Lussier Desbiens*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本文提出MOAR路径规划器，通过风险感知成本函数和自适应速度规划，实现无人机在复杂动态环境中的实时轨迹优化，在安全、时间和能量之间取得平衡。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 无人机巡检需要在靠近障碍物的环境中导航，同时考虑天气、通信可靠性和电池续航等动态风险因素，现有方法难以应对这些 evolving risks。

Method: 提出MOAR路径规划器，包含风险感知成本函数（集成预计算成本图、损伤成本和插入成本概念）、自适应速度规划框架，并在离散状态和动作空间的图中搜索最优路径。

Result: 通过仿真和真实飞行测试验证，生成的实时轨迹在多项评估指标上达到主流算法约90%的性能范围。

Conclusion: MOAR框架能够使无人机在关键任务中实现更自主、更可靠的导航。

Abstract: The problem of autonomous navigation for UAV inspection remains challenging as it requires effectively navigating in close proximity to obstacles, while accounting for dynamic risk factors such as weather conditions, communication reliability, and battery autonomy. This paper introduces the MOAR path planner which addresses the complexities of evolving risks during missions. It offers real-time trajectory adaptation while concurrently optimizing safety, time, and energy. The planner employs a risk-aware cost function that integrates pre-computed cost maps, the new concepts of damage and insertion costs, and an adaptive speed planning framework. With that, the optimal path is searched in a graph using a discrete representation of the state and action spaces. The method is evaluated through simulations and real-world flight tests. The results show the capability to generate real-time trajectories spanning a broad range of evaluation metrics: around 90% of the range occupied by popular algorithms. The proposed framework contributes by enabling UAVs to navigate more autonomously and reliably in critical missions.

</details>


### [488] [Realtime Wind Estimation using Low Cost Quadrotor Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30581)
*Hiranya Udagedara,Mahdis Bisheban*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本文提出使用无迹卡尔曼滤波（UKF）替代扩展卡尔曼滤波（EKF），在SE(3)群上对四旋翼无人机建模，实现高非线性环境下（如 windy 条件）的精确风速估计，同时保持飞行轨迹。仿真结果表明，UKF在非线性增强时始终优于EKF。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有EKF方法依赖对非线性四旋翼动力学的线性化近似，在强风等高非线性场景下精度受限；且多数研究仅适用于悬停或近直线轨迹下的恒定风速估计，缺乏对复杂轨迹和时变风速的支持。

Method: 采用无迹卡尔曼滤波（UKF）作为非线性状态估计器；将四旋翼无人机模型建立在特殊欧几里得群SE(3)上；使用几何控制器维持飞行路径；通过数值仿真对比UKF与EKF在不同非线性程度下的表现。

Result: 随着仿真中非线性程度的增加，UKF始终优于EKF；UKF能够在保持轨迹最小偏差的同时提供准确的风速估计。

Conclusion: UKF在高非线性场景下是一种可靠的风速估计方法，能够替代传统EKF用于四旋翼无人机的实时风速估计与轨迹保持。

Abstract: In environmental monitoring as well as emergency response applications such as wildfires, wind velocity measurement is essential. Quadrotor UAVs have become popular platforms for wind velocity estimation due to their maneuverability, compact size, and cost-effectiveness. Numerous studies use the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) to estimate the wind velocity based on the quadrotor dynamic model. However, most of them use hovering quadrotors only for wind estimation, others use a near-linear trajectory to estimate near-constant velocities. Furthermore, EKF performance is constrained by its reliance on linearized approximations of the nonlinear quadrotor dynamics around current states, limiting accuracy in highly nonlinear scenarios, including windy conditions. This study proposes the use of an Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF), a nonlinear estimator to provide accurate wind estimations while maintaining the trajectory of the quadrotor UAV. The quadrotor is modeled on the Special Euclidean group SE(3) and the approach is evaluated through numerical simulations using a geometric controller to maintain quadrotor flight paths. The results indicate that as the nonlinearity of the simulation increases, the UKF consistently outperforms the EKF. This demonstrates the potential of the UKF as a reliable estimator for highly nonlinear scenarios, capable of maintaining the trajectory with minimal deviation while providing accurate wind velocity estimations.

</details>


### [489] [Sequential Planning via Anchored Robotic Keypoints](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30613)
*Bryce Grant,Aryeh Rothenberg,Logan Senning,Zonghe Chua,Zach Patterson,Peng Wang*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: SPARK是一种无需训练的神经符号操作系统，通过锚定机器人关键点进行序列规划，在LIBERO-PRO任务上达到43.7%的成功率，是CaP-Agent0和VLA基线的两倍以上。它通过类型化行为树组合基元，将计算预算集中用于感知层，并使用恢复循环处理失败，无需重新调用LLM。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有方法（如CaP-Agent0）在每次任务步骤都重新查询LLM，重启式解决方案对策略微小失败的代价高昂；感知层在位置和任务变化下最易失败，需要更高效的感知计算分配。

Method: 1) 一次Gemini调用将计划组合为类型化行为树(BT)，包含已嵌入的低级控制（运动、抓取、深度几何）；2) 第二次Gemini调用为每个对象提出三个替代文本提示，SAM3评估并选择最置信的提示→标签对；3) 恢复循环在原始语失败时重试已失败基元，使用新检测到的对象，无需新的LLM调用。

Result: 在LIBERO-PRO上达到43.7%，超过CaP-Agent0 (18.2%)和VLA基线；替代提示在空间套件上+27.7分，对象套件上+10.0分；恢复循环整体+5.0分；在三个机器人系列、九项任务上平均68%成功率。

Conclusion: SPARK证明了无需训练的神经符号规划器可以超越VLA策略，并且可以为那些策略提供无需遥操作的数据。检测器、规划器和控制器模块可独立替换而无需训练，每个基元的可检查后条件可将故障追溯到对应模块或运动学限制。

Abstract: We present Sequential Planning via Anchored Robotic Keypoints, SPARK, a training-free neurosymbolic manipulation system that reaches 43.7% on six LIBERO-PRO position \& task cells, more than doubling CaP-Agent0 and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) baselines. CaP-Agent0, a multi-turn code-generation agent, achieves 18.2% by re-querying an LLM at every turn, but its restart-from-scratch solution proves costly against minor policy failures. Perception is the layer that fails most under position and task changes so SPARK spends its computation there. A single Gemini call composes the plan as a typed behavior tree (BT) of composable primitives, each already containing the low-level control (motion, grasping, depth geometry) a code-generation agent would otherwise regenerate on every trial. The rest of the budget goes to perception: a second Gemini call proposes three alternative text prompts per object, SAM3 evaluates each, and we keep the prompt$\to$label pair with the most confident detection and a recovery loop then retries a failed primitive against freshly detected objects, with no new LLM call. The alternative prompts add +27.7 points on the spatial suite and +10.0 on the object suite, with the recovery loop adding +5.0 overall. SPARK runs the same primitives on three robot families (UR10e, Franka FR3, bimanual Franka) across nine unique tasks at twenty trials each, averaging 68%. Since the detector, planner, and controller modules sit behind the typed plan, they swap independently without training, and each primitive's checkable post-condition traces a failure to the corresponding module or a kinematic limit. Every trial logs a verified, labeled trajectory, so a training-free planner that already beats VLAs can supply the data those policies need without teleoperation. Project page: https://cwru-aism.github.io/spark-page/

</details>


### [490] [GROW$^2$: Grounding Which and Where for Robot Tool Use](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30632)
*Yuhong Deng,Yuyao Liu,David Hsu*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 提出GROW$^2$框架，通过分层语义-几何推理，利用VLM和视觉基础模型实现机器人开放世界工具使用的零样本能力，在基准和真实实验中优于现有方法。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 机器人需要创造性地使用工具（如用盘子切蛋糕），但面临开放世界可用性定位的挑战：既要选择开放类别的物体作为工具，又要定位其具体作用区域。

Method: 将定位过程分层为语义层和几何层：语义层利用VLM的常识推理解析任务指令、选择工具并识别相关部件；几何层利用视觉基础模型将部件定位到精确的3D区域。

Result: 在可用性预测基准上优于SOTA，在仿真和真实机器人工具使用实验中实现零样本泛化，效果优于基线方法。

Conclusion: 以物体部件作为自然抽象进行分层推理，可有效实现开放世界工具使用的零样本泛化，避免数据密集的端到端训练。

Abstract: Can the robot use a plate to cut a cake if no knife is available? Tool use greatly expands robot capabilities, but to use tools creatively beyond their intended functions, the robot faces the challenge of $\textit{open-world affordance grounding}$: select an open-category object to act as a tool and localize its specific region of action. To this end, we introduce GROW$^2$ (GROunding Which and Where), which leverages object parts as a natural abstraction to split the grounding process hierarchically into semantic and geometric levels, thus bypassing the need for data-heavy, end-to-end training. Semantically, GROW$^2$ harnesses the commonsense reasoning of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to parse a natural-language task instruction, select a suitable object as the tool, and identify task-relevant parts on the tool and the target object. Geometrically, vision foundation models then ground the selected parts into precise 3D regions from a single RGB-D image. Experiments on established benchmarks show that GROW$^2$ outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on affordance prediction benchmarks. Further, it achieves zero-shot generalization over open-category objects and outperforms baselines in both simulated and real-world robot tool use experiments.

</details>


### [491] [VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30645)
*Yen-Jen Wang,Jiaman Li,Sirui Chen,Takara E. Truong,Pei Xu,Pieter Abbeel,Rocky Duan,Koushil Sreenath,Angjoo Kanazawa,Carmelo Sferrazza,Guanya Shi,Karen Liu*

Main category: cs.RO

TL;DR: 本文提出了一种通过合成数据生成视觉-语言-运动学（VLK）监督信号的方法，用于训练人形机器人全身感知-操作策略，并在真实Unitree G1机器人上实现了有效的仿真到现实迁移。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有数据源无法提供同步的自我中心图像、语言指令和机器人运动学轨迹这一完整三元组，限制了基于感知的人形机器人全身操作学习。

Method: 利用3D高斯泼溅重建度量级室内场景，合成导航和物体交互轨迹，渲染对应的自我中心观察，生成48,000条配对轨迹；训练VLK策略预测短时域全身运动学轨迹，并通过全身跟踪器将其转换为物理人形机器人的实际动作。

Result: 在真实Unitree G1机器人上成功完成了导航和单物体运输任务，验证了合成数据在人形机器人全身感知-操作任务中的有效性。

Conclusion: 在重建场景中合成交互轨迹能够为基于感知的人形机器人全身操作提供有效的监督信号，实现从仿真到现实的成功迁移。

Abstract: Perception-based humanoid loco-manipulation requires connecting egocentric observations and task instructions to whole-body motion. Learning this mapping requires synchronized egocentric images, language commands, and robot-compatible kinematic trajectories, yet no existing data source provides this complete tuple at scale. We address this bottleneck by generating vision-language-kinematics (VLK) supervision synthetically in reconstructed scenes. Our pipeline leverages 3D Gaussian Splatting to reconstruct metric-scale indoor environments, synthesizes navigation and object-interaction trajectories using privileged scene information, and renders paired egocentric observations after the fact. We produce 48,000 paired trajectories with no human intervention and train a VLK policy that predicts short-horizon whole-body kinematic trajectories. A whole-body tracker converts these predictions into actions on the physical humanoid. We evaluate on the physical Unitree G1 performing navigation and single-object transport, demonstrating that synthesized interactions in reconstructed scenes provide effective supervision for sim-to-real perception-based humanoid loco-manipulation. Project Website: https://vision-language-kinematics.github.io/

</details>
